meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Complete Projects Faster (Founder Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS build. Master pricing models to validate your MVP, maintain momentum, and generate revenue faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You can't finish projects because you lack market signal. By implementing a concrete SaaS pricing model early, you force validation. When users pay, you stop tinkering and start shipping. Use Antigravity to structure your billing logic and Claude Code to automate the integration, turning your MVP into a revenue machine.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' code that never saw a single dollar. You’re stuck in the loop of infinite feature creep because you haven't given your product a reason to exist outside your IDE. Without a pricing model, you are building a hobby, not a business.

True velocity comes from the tension of a transaction. When you define your pricing, you define your value proposition. This checklist strips away the noise, leveraging modern AI tooling to lock in your revenue architecture so you can stop polishing pixels and start closing customers.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the one unit of value your customer pays for. If you can't measure it, you can't price it. This prevents scope creep by aligning features with revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS MVP core feature and suggest three value-based pricing tiers that align with high-intent customer personas.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze current codebase and suggest a value-based pricing schema based on the core feature set'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a scalable billing architecture. Move away from hardcoded logic to a modular pricing service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular pricing service interface that handles monthly and annual subscriptions via Stripe API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold a new pricing service module that integrates Stripe webhooks and subscription tiers'

steps.title: Implement Frictionless Checkout
steps.description: A complex checkout kills conversions. Use Claude Code to build a 'Buy Now' flow that takes the user from interest to payment in under 60 seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a pricing table that redirects to a Stripe Checkout session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a pricing table component and connect it to the stripe-checkout-session endpoint'

steps.title: Automate Revenue Validation
steps.description: Don't just launch; track. Build a dashboard that shows MRR and user conversion rates to keep you motivated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a SQL query or Prisma schema to track user conversion events from free tier to paid subscriber.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a dashboard view that pulls MRR data from the database and displays it in the admin panel'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Deploy your pricing logic. The moment it's live, your project is 'finished' in the eyes of the market. Now you iterate based on actual data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my deployment pipeline and suggest a canary release strategy for the new pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure a staging environment for testing the new billing integration before production deployment'

pro_tips: Never offer a 'Free' tier; offer a 'Trial' tier. Free users don't provide the signal you need to validate your product.
Price higher than you think. You can always offer a discount later, but raising prices on early adopters is a churn nightmare.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by showing your high-tier enterprise plan first; it makes your mid-tier look like a no-brainer.
Automate your dunning process (failed payment recovery) immediately; it’s the easiest way to save 5-10% of your revenue.
If nobody complains about your price, you are likely too cheap. Iterate until you hit the threshold of moderate resistance.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus entirely to Top-of-Funnel marketing. Your code is finished; now go get your first ten paying customers.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Your SaaS deserves to be a business, and your time is too valuable to spend on features nobody will pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
